data.title: Micron Stock (MU) Hits a New 52-Week High – Will the Rally Continue?
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) stock continues its impressive run, hitting a new 52-week high of $365.81 on January 16, 2026, amid aggressive expansion and booming artificial intelligence (AI) demand. This rally builds on a 231% gain over the past year, raising questions about its continuity in a competitive semiconductor landscape.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AI tailwinds and manufacturing scale have positioned Micron strongly, but risks from potential deal delays, geopolitical tensions in Taiwan, intense rivalry from Samsung (SSNLF) and SK Hynix, and broader economic slowdowns could temper hyperscaler spending.

Key Catalysts Behind Micron's Recent Rally

  • The company broke ground on a massive $100 billion semiconductor manufacturing plant in upstate New York. Micron executives describe this Onondaga County facility near Syracuse as the largest semiconductor plant in the US. It is slated to be fully operational by 2030 and create 50,000 jobs in New York state.
  • Director Liu Teyin M purchased 23,200 common shares on January 13-14, as seen in a Form 4 filing. This move signals high insider confidence in the company's future potential.
  • Micron signed a letter of intent (LOI) to buy a fabrication plant from Taiwan's Powerchip Semiconductor Manufacturing ‌Corp. for $1.8 billion, expected to close in the second half of 2026. Micron will gradually boost DRAM (Dynamic Random Access Memory) production at the site, with major wafer output starting late 2027. Its location near Micron's current Taiwan facilities improves long-term efficiency. Micron and Powerchip will team up on chip assembly post-production, while Micron supports Powerchip's older DRAM lines during the switch.

The massive AI-driven demand from data centers and hyperscalers has sparked memory chip shortages and driven up prices. Micron plans to grow its production capacity via the Taiwan fab purchase and its upcoming New York plant.

Micron Stock Movement

Over the past year, MU shares have soared more than 231%, with a sharp rally toward the end of 2025. This momentum comes from strong demand for Micron's high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and DRAM chips, fueled by AI applications. Moreover, consistent quarterly earnings beats, improving profitability, and rising global memory prices have further boosted the stock.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Tesla (TSLA) Tightens Its Grip on South Korea's EV Market with Aggressive Pricing
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) is strengthening its position in South Korea, one of Asia's most competitive electric vehicle (EV) markets, by slashing prices and triggering a full-scale price war. Recent price cuts on the Model 3 and Model Y have made them more affordable, helping TSLA pull ahead of major rivals in both sales and market share.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

With this move, Korea might be entering a "China-style EV price war," where Tesla keeps cutting prices to protect its market share, pushing rivals to follow suit and pressuring industry profits.

Price Cuts Make Tesla's Vehicles Affordable

Tesla sharply cut the price of the Model 3, lowering the Standard RWD version to 41.99 million won (about $28,500) from 59.9 million won. With subsidies, the final price drops into the low‑30‑million‑won range.

The company also reduced prices on the Model 3 Performance and rolled out discounts on the Model Y, bringing both models below the 40 million won threshold after incentives.

These cuts are significant as most EVs in Korea, especially models from Hyundai (HYMLF), Kia (KIMTF), BMW (BAMXF), and Mercedes (MBGAF), usually cost between 40 million and 50 million won. Thus, Tesla's aggressive pricing suddenly makes its cars some of the most cost-competitive options in the country.

Tesla Sees Strong Sales Surge in 2025

According to market tracker CarIsYou, Tesla's sales in Korea more than doubled last year. The company sold 59,949 units, reflecting a 101.5% increase from the previous year. The Model Y alone sold 50,405 units, becoming the top-selling imported vehicle in the country.

Several factors are driving Tesla's momentum, such as:

  • Affordability: After subsidies, Tesla's pricing now rivals or beats local EVs.
  • Brand appeal: Younger buyers in their 30s and 40s prefer Tesla for its tech-forward image and Autopilot features.
  • Feature advantage: Even with some feature trade-offs in the lower-priced Model 3, Tesla still offers strong value relative to rivals.
  • Market timing: Overall, EV demand has softened in Korea, making price a decisive factor.

Is Tesla a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the last three months. At $397.47, the average Tesla price target implies a 9.15% downside risk. The stock has gained 33.19% over the past six months.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: 'Temporary' Iron Ore Discounts Won't Hit BHP's Pricing Power, Says RBC
data.description:
BHP Group Limited (BHP) (AU:BHP) reported record iron ore production in the first six months of the current fiscal year, while noting it has accepted lower prices during negotiations with China. Nonetheless, RBC five-star analyst Kaan Peker views the discounts as "optical, temporary, and economically bound," noting that they do not signal any deterioration in the mining giant's underlying pricing power. Following the announcement, BHP shares in Australia fell by almost 2% today.

data.title: Why PDD Holdings Stock Has Fallen for Six Straight Sessions
data.description:
Shares of PDD Holdings (PDD), the China-based e-commerce firm, have fallen for six straight sessions, dropping more than 12% since January 8, as investors react to rising regulatory pressure in China.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to a Bloomberg report, Chinese authorities have widened a probe into the company following clashes between PDD employees and regulators last month. Reportedly, regulators have since sent more than 100 investigators to PDD's Shanghai headquarters to carry out on-site checks.

Regulatory Review Beyond Initial Checks

The investigation now covers a wide range of issues, including alleged fake deliveries, tax matters, and other compliance concerns. Regulators have carried out interviews and document reviews at the company's offices as part of the process.

The increased scrutiny is already affecting operations. Marketing plans ahead of the Lunar New Year have slowed, and some projects have been delayed as staff prepare for further inspections and interviews.

Is PDD a Good Stock to Buy Now?

PDD has grown quickly over the past few years, rising from a small startup into one of China's largest online shopping firms. It is now best known outside China as the owner of Temu, which competes with Shein in the U.S. and Europe.

That growth may now be drawing closer attention from regulators. While PDD has not commented on the probe, the growing regulatory scrutiny has weighed on sentiment. For now, investors remain cautious as they assess how much the investigation could disrupt operations and earnings.

Turning to Wall Street, PDD stock carries a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on seven Buys, four Holds, and zero Sell ratings. The average PDD stock price target is $146.39, implying 37% upside potential over the course of 2026.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Lululemon's Founder Targets Advent's Exit in Prolonged Proxy Fight
data.description:
Chip Wilson, founder of athleisure company Lululemon Athletica (LULU), is aiming to oust private equity firm Advent International's representatives from the company's board amid an ongoing proxy fight. According to Semafor, Wilson insists that he will not consider any settlement with the company unless Advent's David Mussafer and Marti Morfitt step down.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Mussafer serves as Lululemon's lead independent director, while Morfitt is the board chair. Wilson is LULU's second-largest shareholder, with a 4.27% stake as of December 2025. He launched a proxy fight in December last year, nominating three independent directors, including former On Running co-CEO Marc Maurer and ESPN's ex-marketing boss, Laura Gentile.

Why Wilson Wants to Oust Advent

Wilson has stated he does not seek a board seat himself but is resolute about removing Advent's influence. Lululemon's business has been struggling amid sluggish sales, a hunt for a new CEO, and a stock price plunge of nearly 50% over the past year. Moreover, Lululemon faces pressure from activist investor Elliott Investment Management, which holds a stake exceeding $1 billion and is pushing for a comprehensive turnaround.

Lululemon and Advent share a long history, with the latter being responsible for taking Lululemon public and making Wilson one of Canada's richest people. However, Wilson now blames Advent's ongoing presence and influence as one of the key reasons for Lululemon's failures. He also blamed Mussafer and Morfitt for Lululemon's failure to make any settlement thus far.

Additionally, Wilson's frustrations have grown due to Advent's poor track record with consumer brands. For instance, haircare company Olaplex (OLPX), another Advent portfolio firm where Mussafer and two other Lululemon board members serve, has seen its stock plummet since its 2022 IPO (initial public offering).

Is LULU a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Analysts remain sidelined on Lululemon currently. On TipRanks, LULU stock has a Hold consensus rating based on one Buy and 21 Hold ratings. The average Lululemon Athletica price target of $208.19 implies 3.1% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Trump's New Tariffs Could Send the UK Spiraling into a Recession
data.description:
President Trump will impose an additional 10% tariff on eight countries, including the United Kingdom, starting on February 1. Afterward, the rate will rise to 25% on June 1. The tariffs will remain in place until the U.S. secures a deal to acquire Greenland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That could send the UK into a recession, according to Capital Economics. The firm estimates that a 25% tariff on top of the country's existing 10% tariff will decrease its gross domestic product (GDP) by 0.75%, equivalent to a $25.15 billion hit. "With the UK economy currently growing by 0.2-0.3pc a quarter, if this hit came all at once it could trigger a recession," said Capital Economics Chief UK Economist Paul Dales.

Starmer Calls for 'Calm Discussion' on Greenland Dispute

Unlike several other European leaders, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has talked down the prospect of retaliatory tariffs and instead proposed a negotiation process. "I think that this can be resolved, and should be resolved, through calm discussion," he said. Starmer added that imposing tariffs is the wrong approach when addressing differences within an alliance and that the people of Greenland should have control over their future.

European Union leaders are scheduled to meet this Thursday for an emergency summit to discuss their options, which include reviving a $103 billion tariff package on U.S. goods that was shelved last year after the two sides reached a trade agreement.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: With Ondas Stock Up Over 500%, Let's Look at Who Owns It
data.description:

The company recently hosted its investor day, where management outlined growth plans and reaffirmed its long-term outlook for the counter-drone market. The company raised its 2026 revenue target to $170 million–$180 million, citing a growing pipeline and improving visibility.

data.title: 4 Things Investors Are Watching as Markets Reopen After the Long Weekend
data.description:
U.S. markets are reopening after a holiday, as investors return to trading following developments that emerged while markets were closed. With no trading on Monday due to the Martin Luther King Jr. holiday, markets now need to catch up on trade headlines, early earnings signals, and interest-rate expectations. Here are the key issues investors are watching as trading resumes.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

1. Trade headlines finally hit markets: Over the weekend, President Donald Trump said the U.S. would impose new tariffs linked to Greenland. He announced a 10% tariff on imports from eight European countries starting February 1, with the rate set to rise to 25% by June if no deal is reached to acquire Greenland. The comments came while U.S. markets were closed, delaying any immediate reaction. As trading resumes, investors are watching whether tariff concerns weigh on global stocks, particularly companies with large international exposure.

2. Earnings take the spotlight: With earnings season picking up, company results are expected to play a bigger role in stock moves than broader macro headlines. Several major companies are set to report, including Netflix (NFLX), Intel (INTC), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), United Airlines (UAL), 3M (MMM), and Charles Schwab (SCHW).

The focus is on company outlooks rather than past results. Investors want to know if Netflix can keep growing subscribers and whether Intel is seeing a pickup in demand. Attention will also be on how Johnson & Johnson's drug business is holding up, and on what United Airlines, 3M, and Charles Schwab say about costs, margins, and client activity.

3. Interest rates remain a key point: Interest rates remain a key factor for markets. Investors are watching the Federal Reserve closely for any hints on the timing of future rate cuts. If rates stay high for longer, growth stocks could remain under pressure. However, a softer message from the Fed could offer some support to the market.

4. AI and chip stocks remain in focus: AI-related stocks continue to draw attention, though expectations remain high. Chipmakers saw a brief boost last week after strong results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM). The company reported a 35% jump in fourth-quarter profit, beating estimates on strong demand for AI chips. Net income rose to NT$505.74 billion (about $16 billion), while revenue increased 25.5% from a year earlier to NT$1.046 trillion ($33.73 billion), also topping expectations.

Now, investors want to see if other tech companies can back up the AI story with real revenue growth, not just optimism.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Joby Aviation vs. Archer Aviation Stock: Confidence vs. Upside in 2026
data.description:
Joby Aviation (JOBY) and Archer Aviation (ACHR) enter 2026 at very different points in the same race, as both are working toward a commercial electric air taxi service. However, the market is treating them very differently: Joby is being rewarded for steady progress and early revenue, while Archer is being valued for potential and optional paths. As a result, the debate for 2026 is simple. One stock offers higher confidence, and the other offers higher upside.

data.title: 'It's Expensive for a Reason,' Says Top Investor About Palantir Stock
data.description:
Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) stock appears to have lost some momentum after its remarkable run. Shares have mostly drifted lower since the company's blowout Q3 earnings in early November, raising the possibility that the AI darling's extremely elevated valuation is finally catching up with it.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The PLTR bears have been predicting a decline for some time now, arguing that there's simply no justification for the company's share price to enjoy a valuation multiple that's so far ahead of its peers. After treading water for the last three months, PLTR's price-to-earnings ratio remains around 400x.

Top investor James Foord understands why those concerns are gaining traction, but he doesn't believe they tell the whole story.

"PLTR looks expensive only if you misjudge its market," declares the 5-star investor, who is among the top 2% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

Foord identifies three phases of the AI revolution: compute, infrastructure, and software. He claims that value has been moving up the chain, starting with chips and GPUs and then shifting to data centers and their supporting features. Now, he believes the time has come for software to reign supreme, and Palantir is one of the best positioned to ride the next iteration of the AI wave.

"AI has matured, and as such, it's time to invest in the companies that are actually creating profit for it, not just spending billions laying down the infrastructure," states Foord.

On that note, the investor claims that Palantir has one of the largest TAMs around, with defense spending ballooning and the enterprise software market expected to reach $500 billion by 2030.

That makes Palantir "uniquely positioned" to enjoy the spoils up ahead. With its first-mover advantage and subsequent high costs of switching, Palantir could even replicate the success of the first operating systems.

"Palantir is a clear winner, priced as such, but in my opinion, still very much worth holding for the long term as growth and earnings compound," concludes Foord.

The investor is putting his money where his mouth is, rating PLTR a Strong Buy. (To watch Foord's track record, click here)

Wall Street isn't quite as ready to stake such a strong claim. With 5 Buys, 10 Holds, and 2 Sells, PLTR carries a consensus Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. Its 12-month average price target of $192.88 points to an upside of ~13% from current levels. (See PLTR stock analysis)
data.author: Gabe Ross
